finding_results.notes: Replaced the abstract intimacy summary with a concrete remembered beat: involuntary grip, Kai's felt forearm lock, Lena's immediate physical intervention, and the present-state residue that keeps the memory active in the cafe scene.

The chapter now states plainly that the public release includes the virus-analysis files plus a de-identified biometric archive from Yusuf's files; Meridian's own records remain closed; the inquiry is the specific mechanism that could force those closed records open.
Compressed the wrist beat to two sentences and kept it as lived sensation only. The healing-speed timeline and graft-healing recap are gone.
Kept the towel snag and the two-finger check, and removed the explanatory clause about the port's function and patch installation.

The phone reflex is shown without mechanism recap, Lena's swim disruption is rendered in shorthand rather than tutorial explanation, and the Farah/data beat now clarifies access boundaries without re-teaching enhancement mechanics.
Removed the wrist-healing timeline entirely and reduced the proprioceptive-gap reference to a single demonstrated action.
Post-revision count is 1 prose line with an em dash, reserved for live interruption in dialogue.

finding_results.notes: Rebuilt sentence openings across Lena and Margot's sections. Post-revision count is 6 sentence-openers beginning with 'She'.
Removed the chapter's recurring 'the way' construction entirely. Post-revision count is 0.

Chapter 18 no longer adds a conflicting material label and now uses the continuity-safe term 'small circular port.' The manuscript-wide contradiction remains only because earlier chapters named incompatible materials and were out of scope for this pass.

Rewrote three Margot speeches in the Helga therapy exchange. Line 95: replaced thesis formulation ('The wanting is the experience' / 'the craving and the fix at the same time') with body-concrete description of plugging in — heartbeat in sternum, diaphragm matching, fear of loss arriving in the first second. Line 101: replaced curated memory speech ('proprioceptive sense of being twenty-one and built for exactly this') with rougher, present-tense body recall — hamstrings loading, eyes locking, self-interruption ('His whole body was just —' She stopped), and a concrete physical proof ('My hands are sweating'). Line 109: replaced aphoristic coexistence diagnosis ('These two things just sit next to each other in my head without canceling out') with blunt scenario declaration ('if you plugged me in right now I'd say yes. I know what it cost him and I'd still say yes'). Line 119 ('Like furniture') left intact as character-true blunt Margot. All revised speeches deliver core facts (live feed vs. recorded, intact memory, craving unchanged) through body-reactive language and rougher syntax rather than composed self-interpretation. Preserved Margot's high contraction level, self-interruption tendency, and flat declarative stress register per style bible.

pass_summaries.summary: All chapter-local structural/craft findings were addressed in chapter_18.md, including the concrete intimacy beat, the clarified Farah/data payoff, the wrist and port compression, and the chapter-level tic reductions. The only remaining partial is the manuscript-wide port-material consistency issue, which Chapter 18 now neutralizes but cannot fully resolve without editing earlier chapters outside this pass.

Single finding addressed: Margot's therapy dialogue replaced composed thesis speech with body-concrete, pressured delivery across three speeches (lines 95, 101, 109). Sensory detail now arrives through re-experienced sensation (heartbeat, hamstring load, sweating hands, spinal compression) rather than articulate addiction diagnosis. Self-interruptions and flat declarations replace balanced aphorisms. No changes to chapter structure, continuity, or non-dialogue passages. Heading contract preserved.
